sentences.sentence: Psychologists have long held that a person's character cannot undergo a transformation in any meaningful way and that the key traits of personality are determined at a very young age.
sentences.translation: 心理学家长期以来一直认为，人的性格不可能发生任何有意义的转变，而且人格的关键特质在很小的时候就已确定。

structure.text: that a person's character cannot undergo a transformation in any meaningful way
and that the key traits of personality are determined at a very young age

difficult_points: 两个由 that 引导的宾语从句以 and 并列，共同作 held 的宾语。
are determined 是被动语态，表示人格特质在很小的年龄就已形成。

sentences.sentence: However, researchers have begun looking more closely at ways we can change.
sentences.translation: 然而，研究人员已经开始更仔细地考察我们能够改变的方式。

structure.text: have begun looking more closely at ways

difficult_points: have begun looking 使用现在完成时，强调这一研究趋势已经开始并延续至今。
we can change 是省略关系副词 in which 的定语从句，修饰 ways。

sentences.sentence: Positive psychologists have identified 24 qualities we admire, such as loyalty and kindness, and are studying them to find out why they come so naturally to some people.
sentences.translation: 积极心理学家已经确定了忠诚、善良等24种受人赞赏的品质，并正在研究这些品质，以弄清它们为何会自然而然地出现在某些人身上。

structure.text: to find out why they come so naturally to some people

difficult_points: we admire 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 24 qualities。
why they come so naturally to some people 是 find out 的宾语从句，其中 they 指这些品质。

sentences.sentence: What they're discovering is that many of these qualities amount to habitual behaviour that determines the way we respond to the world.
sentences.translation: 他们正在发现的是，其中许多品质其实就是习惯性行为，而这种行为决定了我们回应世界的方式。

structure.text: that many of these qualities amount to habitual behaviour
that determines the way we respond to the world

difficult_points: What they're discovering 是 what 引导的主语从句，整体表示“他们正在发现的事情”。
第二个 that 引导定语从句修饰 habitual behaviour，其中 we respond to the world 又修饰 the way。

sentences.sentence: The good news is that all this can be learned.
sentences.translation: 好消息是，这一切都是可以学会的。

structure.text: that all this can be learned

difficult_points: that 从句作表语，具体说明好消息的内容。
can be learned 是情态动词加被动语态，强调这些品质可以通过学习获得。

sentences.sentence: Some qualities are less challenging to develop than others, optimism being one of them.
sentences.translation: 有些品质比其他品质更容易培养，乐观就是其中之一。

structure.text: are less challenging to develop than others

difficult_points: less challenging ... than ... 是比较结构，意思是培养某些品质的难度更低。
optimism being one of them 是独立主格结构，补充说明乐观属于较容易培养的品质。

sentences.sentence: However, developing qualities requires mastering a range of skills which are diverse and sometimes surprising.
sentences.translation: 不过，培养这些品质需要掌握一系列多样且有时出人意料的技能。

structure.text: which are diverse and sometimes surprising

difficult_points: developing qualities 是动名词短语作主语，mastering ... 是动名词短语作 requires 的宾语。
which 引导非限定性定语从句，说明这些技能具有多样且有时意外的特点。

sentences.sentence: For example, to bring more joy and passion into your life, you must be open to experiencing negative emotions.
sentences.translation: 例如，要让生活中拥有更多快乐与激情，你必须愿意敞开心扉去体验负面情绪。

structure.text: to bring more joy and passion into your life
must be open to experiencing negative emotions

difficult_points: 句首不定式短语表示目的，即为了给生活带来更多快乐和激情。
be open to 中 to 是介词，因此后接动名词 experiencing，而不是动词原形。

expressions.meaning_zh: 愿意接受；对……持开放态度

sentences.sentence: Cultivating such qualities will help you realise your full potential.
sentences.translation: 培养这样的品质将帮助你充分发挥自己的潜能。
difficult_points: Cultivating such qualities 是动名词短语作主语。
help you realise 使用 help 后接不带 to 的不定式，realise 在此意为“实现、发挥”。

sentences.sentence: 'The evidence is good that most personality traits can be altered,' says Christopher Peterson, professor of psychology at the University of Michigan, who cites himself as an example.
sentences.translation: “有充分证据表明，大多数人格特质都可以改变，”密歇根大学心理学教授 Christopher Peterson 说道，他还以自己为例。

structure.text: that most personality traits can be altered
professor of psychology at the University of Michigan
who cites himself as an example

difficult_points: that 从句具体说明 evidence 的内容，can be altered 是被动语态。
professor ... 是 Christopher Peterson 的同位语，who 从句继续补充说明他。

sentences.sentence: Inherently introverted, he realised early on that as an academic, his reticence would prove disastrous in the lecture hall.
sentences.translation: 他天生内向，却很早便意识到，作为一名学者，他的沉默寡言在讲堂上会带来灾难性的后果。

structure.text: that as an academic, his reticence would prove disastrous in the lecture hall

difficult_points: Inherently introverted 是句首形容词短语，说明主语 he 天生内向。
prove 后接形容词 disastrous 作主语补足语，表示“结果证明会是灾难性的”。

sentences.sentence: So he learned to be more outgoing and to entertain his classes.
sentences.translation: 于是，他学会了变得更加外向，也学会了活跃课堂、吸引学生。
difficult_points: 两个由 and 连接的不定式共同作 learned 的补足成分。
entertain his classes 不是单纯“娱乐课堂”，而是指让课堂生动并吸引学生。

sentences.sentence: 'Now my extroverted behaviour is spontaneous,' he says.
sentences.translation: “现在，我的外向表现已经自然而然了，”他说。
difficult_points: spontaneous 在这里表示这种外向行为已经无需刻意练习便会自然出现。
he says 是直接引语后的报道语，说明说话者。

sentences.sentence: David Fajgenbaum had to make a similar transition.
sentences.translation: David Fajgenbaum 也不得不经历类似的转变。
difficult_points: had to 表示过去由于现实需要而不得不采取行动。
a similar transition 承接上一段 Peterson 改变行为方式的经历。

sentences.sentence: He was preparing for university, when he had an accident that put an end to his sports career.
sentences.translation: 他正在为上大学做准备时遭遇了一场事故，这场事故终结了他的运动生涯。

structure.text: that put an end to his sports career

difficult_points: was preparing 表示事故发生时正在进行的背景动作。
that 引导定语从句修饰 an accident，并说明事故造成的结果。

sentences.sentence: On campus, he quickly found that beyond ordinary counselling, the university had no services for students who were undergoing physical rehabilitation and suffering from depression like him.
sentences.translation: 到了校园后，他很快发现，除了一般的心理咨询，学校没有为像他这样正接受身体康复治疗并饱受抑郁困扰的学生提供服务。

structure.text: that beyond ordinary counselling, the university had no services for students
who were undergoing physical rehabilitation and suffering from depression like him

difficult_points: that 引导 found 的宾语从句，beyond ordinary counselling 表示“除了一般咨询之外”。
who 从句修饰 students，其中 undergoing 和 suffering 是两个并列的进行时谓语。

sentences.sentence: He therefore launched a support group to help others in similar situations.
sentences.translation: 因此，他发起了一个互助小组，帮助其他处境相似的人。

structure.text: to help others in similar situations

difficult_points: therefore 表明成立小组是针对上一句所述服务缺失而采取的行动。
to help ... 是目的状语，说明发起互助小组的目的。

sentences.sentence: He took action despite his own pain - a typical response of an optimist.
sentences.translation: 他不顾自身的痛苦采取了行动——这是乐观主义者的典型反应。

structure.text: a typical response of an optimist

difficult_points: despite 是介词，后接名词短语 his own pain，表达让步关系。
破折号后的名词短语对前面整个行动作评价，说明这是乐观者的典型反应。

sentences.sentence: Suzanne Segerstrom, professor of psychology at the University of Kentucky, believes that the key to increasing optimism is through cultivating optimistic behaviour, rather than positive thinking.
sentences.translation: 肯塔基大学心理学教授 Suzanne Segerstrom 认为，提升乐观程度的关键在于培养乐观行为，而不是进行积极思考。

structure.text: professor of psychology at the University of Kentucky
that the key to increasing optimism is through cultivating optimistic behaviour, rather than positive thinking

difficult_points: professor ... 是插入的同位语，补充说明 Suzanne Segerstrom 的身份。
rather than 连接 cultivating optimistic behaviour 与 positive thinking，突出行为而非单纯思想。

sentences.sentence: She recommends you train yourself to pay attention to good fortune by writing down three positive things that come about each day.
sentences.translation: 她建议你每天写下三件发生的好事，以此训练自己关注好运。

structure.text: you train yourself to pay attention to good fortune
by writing down three positive things

difficult_points: recommends 后省略 that，you train ... 是宾语从句，使用原形 train 表示建议。
by writing down ... 表示训练的方法，that 从句修饰 three positive things。

sentences.sentence: This will help you convince yourself that favourable outcomes actually happen all the time, making it easier to begin taking action.
sentences.translation: 这会帮助你相信，有利的结果其实一直都在发生，从而让开始行动变得更容易。

structure.text: that favourable outcomes actually happen all the time
making it easier to begin taking action

difficult_points: that 从句作 convince yourself 的内容补足成分，说明要让自己相信什么。
making ... 是现在分词短语表示结果，其中 it 是形式宾语，真正内容是 to begin taking action。

sentences.sentence: You can recognise a person who is passionate about a pursuit by the way they are so strongly involved in it.
sentences.translation: 从一个人如此全情投入某项追求的方式中，你就能看出他是否对此充满热情。

structure.text: who is passionate about a pursuit
by the way they are so strongly involved in it

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句修饰 a person，说明所识别的人具有何种特征。
they 是指代单数 a person 的性别中立用法，it 指代 a pursuit。

sentences.sentence: Tanya Streeter's passion is freediving – the sport of plunging deep into the water without tanks or other breathing equipment.
sentences.translation: Tanya Streeter 热爱的项目是自由潜水——一种不借助气瓶或其他呼吸设备深入水下的运动。

structure.text: the sport of plunging deep into the water without tanks or other breathing equipment

difficult_points: 破折号后的名词短语是 freediving 的同位语，对这项运动作解释。
of plunging ... 中 plunging 是动名词，without 短语说明潜水时不使用的设备。

sentences.sentence: Beginning in 1998, she set nine world records and can hold her breath for six minutes.
sentences.translation: 她从1998年开始从事这项运动，创下了九项世界纪录，并且能够屏息六分钟。

structure.text: and can hold her breath for six minutes

difficult_points: Beginning in 1998 是现在分词短语，其逻辑主语是 she，交代起始时间。
set 与 can hold 时态形式不同：前者陈述已创纪录，后者陈述当前能力。

sentences.sentence: The physical stamina required for this sport is intense but the psychological demands are even more overwhelming.
sentences.translation: 这项运动对体能的要求很高，但其心理压力甚至更令人难以承受。

structure.text: The physical stamina required for this sport
but the psychological demands are even more overwhelming

difficult_points: required for this sport 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 physical stamina。
but 连接两个对比分句，even more 突出心理要求比体能要求更难承受。

sentences.sentence: Streeter learned to untangle her fears from her judgment of what her body and mind could do.
sentences.translation: Streeter 学会了把自己的恐惧与对身心能力的理性判断区分开来。

structure.text: to untangle her fears from her judgment
of what her body and mind could do

difficult_points: untangle A from B 在此为比喻用法，表示把恐惧与判断区分开。
what her body and mind could do 是 what 引导的名词性从句，作 of 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: 'In my career as a competitive freediver, there was a limit to what I could do - but it wasn't anywhere near what I thought it was,' she says.
sentences.translation: “在我的自由潜水竞技生涯中，我的能力确实有极限——但那个极限远没有我原以为的那么近，”她说。

structure.text: but it wasn't anywhere near what I thought it was

difficult_points: what I could do 是 what 引导的名词性从句，说明她的能力范围。
it 指代 limit，what I thought it was 表示“我原以为极限所在的位置”。

sentences.sentence: Finding a pursuit that excites you can improve anyone's life.
sentences.translation: 找到一项令自己兴奋的追求，能够改善任何人的生活。
difficult_points: Finding ... 是动名词短语作主语。
that excites you 是定语从句修饰 a pursuit，而不是修饰 Finding。

sentences.sentence: The secret about consuming passions, though, according to psychologist Paul Silvia of the University of North Carolina, is that they require discipline, hard work and ability, which is why they are so rewarding.
sentences.translation: 不过，北卡罗来纳大学心理学家 Paul Silvia 指出，全情投入的热爱之所以特别，在于它需要自律、努力和能力，而这也正是它如此有回报的原因。

structure.text: according to psychologist Paul Silvia of the University of North Carolina
is that they require discipline, hard work and ability
which is why they are so rewarding

difficult_points: though 和 according to ... 都是插入成分，句子主干是 The secret ... is that ...。
which 指代前面“需要自律、努力和能力”这一事实，并引出它们带来回报的原因。

sentences.sentence: Psychologist Todd Kashdan has this advice for those people taking up a new passion: 'As a newcomer, you also have to tolerate and laugh at your own ignorance.
sentences.translation: 心理学家 Todd Kashdan 给刚开始培养新爱好的人这样的建议：“作为新手，你还必须容忍并笑对自己的无知。

structure.text: for those people taking up a new passion
you also have to tolerate and laugh at your own ignorance

difficult_points: taking up a new passion 是现在分词短语，后置修饰 those people。
tolerate 和 laugh at 是 have to 后的并列动词，共同以 your own ignorance 为语义对象。

expressions.meaning_zh: 开始培养一项新的爱好

sentences.sentence: You must be willing to accept the negative feelings that come your way,' he says.
sentences.translation: 你必须愿意接受随之而来的负面感受，”他说。

structure.text: must be willing to accept the negative feelings

difficult_points: be willing to 表示主动作好接受某种经历的心理准备。
that 从句修饰 negative feelings，come your way 表示某事来到你面前或发生在你身上。

expressions.meaning_zh: 发生在你身上；出现在你面前

sentences.sentence: In 2004, physician-scientist Mauro Zappaterra began his PhD research at Harvard Medical School.
sentences.translation: 2004年，医学科学家 Mauro Zappaterra 在哈佛医学院开始了博士研究。
difficult_points: physician-scientist 是复合名词，在姓名前说明 Zappaterra 的职业身份。
In 2004 与 at Harvard Medical School 分别交代时间和地点。

sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, he was miserable as his research wasn't compatible with his curiosity about healing.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，他非常痛苦，因为自己的研究与他对治疗的好奇心并不相容。

structure.text: as his research wasn't compatible with his curiosity about healing

difficult_points: as 在这里引导原因状语从句，相当于 because，而不是表示时间。
be compatible with 表示两者相容，此处否定说明研究方向与他的兴趣不吻合。

sentences.sentence: He finally took a break and during eight months in Santa Fe, Zappaterra learned about alternative healing techniques not taught at Harvard.
sentences.translation: 他最终暂停了研究；在圣菲的八个月里，Zappaterra 学习了哈佛没有教授的替代疗法。

structure.text: and during eight months in Santa Fe, Zappaterra learned about alternative healing techniques

difficult_points: and 连接两个完整分句，第二个分句重新用 Zappaterra 作主语。
not taught at Harvard 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 alternative healing techniques。

sentences.sentence: When he got back, he switched labs to study how cerebrospinal fluid nourishes the developing nervous system.
sentences.translation: 回来后，他换了实验室，转而研究脑脊液如何滋养发育中的神经系统。

structure.text: to study how cerebrospinal fluid nourishes the developing nervous system

difficult_points: When 从句交代他改变研究方向的时间背景。
to study ... 表示换实验室的目的，其中 how 从句作 study 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: He also vowed to look for the joy in everything, including failure, as this could help him learn about his research and himself.
sentences.translation: 他还发誓要在一切事物中寻找乐趣，包括失败，因为这样能帮助他更加了解自己的研究和自身。

structure.text: to look for the joy in everything
as this could help him learn about his research and himself

difficult_points: to look for ... 作 vowed 的补足成分，说明他立誓要做的事。
as 引导原因状语从句，this 指代在包括失败的一切事物中寻找乐趣。

sentences.sentence: One thing that can hold joy back is a person's concentration on avoiding failure rather than their looking forward to doing something well.
sentences.translation: 会阻碍快乐的一件事，是一个人只专注于避免失败，而不是期待把某件事做好。

structure.text: is a person's concentration on avoiding failure
rather than their looking forward to doing something well

difficult_points: that 从句修饰 One thing，hold joy back 表示阻碍快乐出现。
their looking forward ... 是带物主代词的动名词结构，与 avoiding failure 形成对比。

sentences.sentence: 'Focusing on being safe might get in the way of your reaching your goals,' explains Kashdan.
sentences.translation: “一心只求稳妥，可能会妨碍你实现目标，”Kashdan 解释道。

structure.text: might get in the way of

difficult_points: Focusing on being safe 是动名词短语作主语。
your reaching your goals 是物主代词加动名词结构，作介词 of 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: For example, are you hoping to get through a business lunch without embarrassing yourself, or are you thinking about how fascinating the conversation might be?
sentences.translation: 例如，参加商务午餐时，你是只希望顺利熬过去而不让自己出丑，还是在想这场谈话可能会多么有趣？

structure.text: to get through a business lunch without embarrassing yourself
how fascinating the conversation might be

difficult_points: or 连接两个选择疑问分句，对比避免出丑与期待精彩交流两种关注点。
how fascinating ... might be 是感叹程度型的名词性从句，作 about 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Usually, we think of courage in physical terms but ordinary life demands something else.
sentences.translation: 通常，我们从身体层面理解勇气，但日常生活所需要的是另一种勇气。

structure.text: we think of courage in physical terms
but ordinary life demands something else

difficult_points: but 连接两个对比鲜明的分句，区分身体层面的勇气与日常生活所需的勇气。
something else 没有直接重复 courage，而是指另一种形式的勇气。

sentences.sentence: For marketing executive Kenneth Pedeleose, it meant speaking out against something he thought was ethically wrong.
sentences.translation: 对营销主管 Kenneth Pedeleose 来说，这意味着公开反对他认为在道德上错误的事情。
difficult_points: it 回指上一句所说的日常生活中的勇气。
he thought was ethically wrong 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 something。

sentences.sentence: The new manager was intimidating staff so Pedeleose carefully recorded each instance of bullying and eventually took the evidence to a senior director, knowing his own job security would be threatened.
sentences.translation: 新经理一直在恐吓员工，因此 Pedeleose 仔细记录了每一次欺凌行为，最终把证据交给一位高级主管，尽管他知道自己的工作保障会受到威胁。

structure.text: The new manager was intimidating staff
so Pedeleose carefully recorded each instance of bullying
and eventually took the evidence to a senior director
knowing his own job security would be threatened

difficult_points: so 引出 Pedeleose 针对经理行为采取的结果性行动，and 再连接他的第二项行动。
knowing ... 是现在分词短语表示伴随情况，其中 would be threatened 是过去将来时的被动语态。

sentences.sentence: Eventually the manager was the one to go.
sentences.translation: 最终，离开的反而是那位经理。
difficult_points: the one to go 表示“那个必须离开的人”，to go 是不定式后置修饰 the one。
Eventually 强调结果与 Pedeleose 担忧自己失业的情况形成反转。

sentences.sentence: According to Cynthia Pury, a psychologist at Clemson University, Pedeleose's story proves the point that courage is not motivated by fearlessness, but by moral obligation.
sentences.translation: 克莱姆森大学心理学家 Cynthia Pury 认为，Pedeleose 的故事证明了一点：勇气并非由无所畏惧驱动，而是源于道德责任。

structure.text: that courage is not motivated by fearlessness, but by moral obligation

difficult_points: a psychologist ... 是 Cynthia Pury 的同位语，说明其身份。
that 从句说明 the point 的具体内容，not ... but ... 对比无畏与道德责任。

sentences.sentence: Pury also believes that people can acquire courage.
sentences.translation: Pury 还认为，人们可以后天获得勇气。
difficult_points: that 引导 believes 的宾语从句。
acquire 在此强调勇气并非固定天赋，而是可以后天培养获得。

sentences.sentence: Many of her students said that faced with a risky situation, they first tried to calm themselves down, then looked for a way to mitigate the danger, just as Pedeleose did by documenting his allegations.
sentences.translation: 她的许多学生说，面对危险情境时，他们会先努力让自己冷静下来，然后寻找减轻危险的方法，就像 Pedeleose 通过记录自己的指控所做的那样。

structure.text: that faced with a risky situation, they first tried to calm themselves down
then looked for a way to mitigate the danger
just as Pedeleose did by documenting his allegations

difficult_points: faced with ... 是过去分词短语，表示学生处于某种危险情境中。
did 代替前文所述的应对危险行为，by documenting ... 说明 Pedeleose 采取的方法。

sentences.sentence: Over the long term, picking up a new character trait may help you move toward being the person you want to be.
sentences.translation: 从长远来看，培养一种新的性格特质可能会帮助你逐渐成为自己想成为的人。

structure.text: picking up a new character trait
being the person you want to be

difficult_points: picking up ... 是动名词短语作主语，指逐渐学会或养成一种新特质。
you want to be 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 the person。

expressions.text: pick up a new character trait
expressions.meaning_zh: 培养一种新的性格特质

sentences.sentence: And in the short term, the effort itself could be surprisingly rewarding, a kind of internal adventure.
sentences.translation: 而从短期来看，这种努力本身也可能出乎意料地令人满足，仿佛一场内在的冒险。
difficult_points: in the short term 与上一句的 Over the long term 形成时间尺度上的对照。
a kind of internal adventure 是补充性同位语，把培养性格的努力比作内在冒险。
